records.ety: [L. edictum, fr. edicere, edictum, to declare, proclaim; e out + dicere to say: cf. F. dit. See Diction.]

defs: A public command or ordinance by the sovereign power; the proclamation of a law made by an absolute authority, as if by the very act of announcement; a decree; as, the edicts of the Roman emperors; the edicts of the French monarch.
quotes: It stands as an edict in destiny.
